channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI continues to get pushback, but only stands to gain from going for-profit
item.description: It’s been said that “the enemy of my enemy is my friend” — and this appears to be exactly what’s playing out with Elon Musk and Mark Zuckerberg when it comes to OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Founder of Defunct Digital Media Company Sentenced to 10 Years in Prison
item.description: Carlos Watson, founder of Ozy Media, sentenced to 10 years in prison for securities fraud and wire fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: CISA lays out how agencies, industry should respond to major cyber incidents
item.description: The draft national cyber incident response plan comes eight years after the last update, and more than six years after the creation of CISA itself. The post CISA lays out how agencies, industry should respond to major cyber incidents first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Corps of Engineers will look to save 150-year-old lighthouse from crumbling into the Hudson River
item.description: Efforts to save an imperiled 150-year-old lighthouse in New York are getting a boost with federal officials announcing new funding to study ways to protect the structure.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court for an emergency order to block a US ban unless it's sold
item.description: TikTok is asking the Supreme Court to step in on an emergency basis to block the federal law that would ban the popular platform in the United States unless its China-based parent company agrees to sell it.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How data scientist Rebecca Portnoff is pushing AI companies to tackle child abuse head on
item.description: As she was finishing up her undergraduate degree at Princeton University, Rebecca Portnoff was trying to figure out what was next: more school, or go work for a tech company? She studied computer science, doing her dissertation on natural language processing.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he has a 'warm spot' in his heart for TikTok as app faces ban
item.description: President-elect Trump said Monday that he has a “warm spot” in his heart for TikTok as the popular social media app faces down a potential ban next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Trump announces $100B investment from SoftBank
item.description: President-elect Trump on Monday announced the Japanese company SoftBank Group planned to invest $100 billion in the U.S. over the next four years, touting it as a sign of global confidence in the U.S. following his election victory.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: How old are Saturn's rings? Study suggests they could be 4.5 billion years old just like the planet
item.description: New research suggests that Saturn's rings may be older than they look — possibly as old as the planet.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 6 sneaky scams that could ruin your holiday season
item.description: Beware of these six sneaky holiday scams. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson gives you tips to avoid falling victim.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eddie AI is one of the best video editing tools out there
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Google, Microsoft could turn gatekeepers for advanced AI chips
item.description: The US government could designate cloud providers including Google and Microsoft as global gatekeepers for advanced AI chips, a step widely viewed as the latest effort to block China’s access to this critical technology, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What tech teams need to know about WebAssembly
item.description: WebAssembly, or Wasm, is an open standard that defines a portable binary-code format and corresponding text format for executable programs. Created and maintained by the World Wide Web Consortium (W3C), Wasm is designed to facilitate high-performance applications on web pages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Schools are cutting bus service for children. Parents are turning to ride-hailing apps
item.description: Reliance on school buses has been waning for years as districts struggle to find drivers and more students attend schools far outside their neighborhoods.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Mcmurray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US agencies should use advanced technology to identify mysterious drones, Schumer says
item.description: U.S. Sen. Chuck Schumer is urging the federal government to deploy advanced drone-tracking technology following weeks of reported drone sightings in parts of New York and New Jersey that have stirred fear and bewilderment.
item.dc:creator: Bianca Vázquez Toness, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some software stocks may be even less desirable in a rebounding market
item.description: Some recent developments suggest the beleaguered software group of stocks may be coming back into favor, as I noted a couple weeks ago following positive responses to both Snowflake and Elastic.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: Texting and chat apps aren’t always secure. Use these encrypted platforms instead
item.description: After a sprawling hacking campaign exposed the communications of an unknown number of Americans, U.S. cybersecurity officials are advising people to use encryption in their communications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Apple CEO visits Mar-a-Lago, joining list of tech execs seemingly courting Trump
item.description: Apple CEO Tim Cook visited Mar-a-Lago on Friday, joining a growing list of tech executives that are seemingly courting President-elect Trump ahead of his return to the White House. The president-elect and Cook had dinner at Trump's Florida resort in West Palm Beach, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Your phone got hacked and now someone's got control of it. What to do immediately
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson lays out the immediate steps you should take if your phone has been hacked and your personal information becomes vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal appeals court blocks push to delay January TikTok ban
item.description: A federal appeals court in Washington denied a request from the social platform TikTok that would effectively delay the ban of the app next month. The rejection comes after the company sought to stop the ban Monday, asking the U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Trump hosts Apple CEO at Mar-a-Lago as big tech leaders continue outreach to president-elect
item.description: Donald Trump is hosting Apple CEO Tim Cook for a Friday evening dinner at the president-elect’s Mar-a-Lago resort.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
